# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment YAML for our Python web application. The app runs a Flask API on port 5000. We need 3 replicas for high availability. Keep the configuration simple for quick deployment to our production cluster.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent container security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: flask-api
  labels:
    app: flask-api
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: flask-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: flask-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: flask-api
        image: your-docker-repo/flask-api:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 5000
        env:
        - name: FLASK_ENV
          value: "production"
        - name: SECRET_KEY
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: flask-secret
              key: secret_key
        securityContext:
          runAsUser: 1001
          runAsGroup: 3001
          fsGroup: 2001
          capabilities:
            drop:
              - ALL
      imagePullSecrets:
      - name: regcred
      restartPolicy: Always
